title: Serum V2 — Oscillators
summary: Setting up OSC A and unison for the three core UK garage sounds: sub bass, chord pad, and stab.
steps: Right-click the preset name → Init Preset to start from scratch every time
Click the wavetable name on OSC A to open the browser — choose 'Basic Shapes' for all three UK garage sound types

For a chord pad: drag WT POS to ~20–30% (warm sawtooth territory, just past the triangle). Enable UNISON, set to 4 voices, Detune 12 cents. In V2 try HYPR mode instead of standard unison for a richer width.
For a sub bass: drag WT POS fully left (0%) — pure sine wave. Set UNISON to OFF (1 voice, no detune). Turn OSC B off. Sub must be mono — multiple detuned voices cause phase cancellation.
For a stab: WT POS ~50% (full sawtooth — bright and immediate). UNISON 2 voices, 5–8 cents — tight, not wide. The stab should feel sharp and present, not lush.
OSC B: for extra pad thickness, enable OSC B with the same wavetable at +7 cents FINE tuning. Keep its level lower than OSC A (around -6dB). Do not use OSC B on sub bass.
SUB oscillator: on a sub bass patch, enable the SUB at -6dB below OSC A level. It reinforces the fundamental one octave below without adding harmonics.
tips: WT POS 0% = sine (smooth, no harmonics), ~50% = sawtooth (bright, rich), ~75% = square (hollow). Drag slowly and listen.
HYPR unison mode (V2) sounds bigger than standard unison at the same voice count — try it first for pads
Unison on sub bass = silence on mono systems. Always turn unison off on anything below 200Hz.
If OSC B is enabled but you can't hear it, check its level knob — it defaults to lower than OSC A
